I'm going to show you why, but first the method behind this matters. When you set it to conversations should be open explicitly, this is built into your flows and your automations. This is done through an action block. This is a trigger. So you can set your flows, you can set a trigger in your flows, in your automations that if the user does this then I want it to open a conversation. Now why I don't build my flows like this is because I want to have an overview of what's happening. I like to lurk in the dark. I like to just check out my live chat and see what's happening. See if there are any buttons are broken, if people actually use the flow, how I build it and that will not be able if you set it to conversations should be open explicitly. This is one of my clients, a dive operator in the South Pacific and we get a lot of inquiries through our live chat, through our page, through email and where we use the ref URL to get people into Messenger because Messenger for us for the dive operator is a crucial element for customer service, building report, humanize the brand before we actually

## [03:06 - 06:06]

sell our products to them. As you can see, the live chat behavior here is set to any message starts a conversation. Now I'm going to show you what that looks like in the live chat. There we go. Every action that people take in the flows is recorded here. Every action that means not only questions asked or messages sent to us, but also downloads. That is the main reason why I prefer to set my live chat settings to any message start a conversation. There's no right, there's no wrong, that's just my preference and the explanation, the reason behind the method behind the methods. Now let's have a look at live chat because this is a pretty awesome tool and I'm going to use my own contact record to show you what all is possible. So as soon as somebody is entered into the live chat, we can select assign one of our team members. So in this case, I'm going to assign it to Chris, who will now receive a message in his Messenger that one of the team members assigned this conversation to Chris. So this will be the queue for Chris to jump in, whether it's through his iPhone or mobile device or desktop, it doesn't really matter, he gets the notification, he can jump in and he can take the conversation from there. Now the cool thing is when I open this up, when Chris sees this or when I see this, when I open up the contact record from a user in the live chat, I can exactly see which tags are applied to that user, which gives me a sense of how active he is or she is or what he or she is interested in because I should have a basic understanding what tags are applied in what flows. And this is also a good reason why you should have a proper naming convention for your tags to make it easy to understand what tags, what the purpose is of a specific tag to get an idea of, to get a bird's eye view of what the user, the steps the user took before they actually came to you in the live chat.

## [06:06 - 09:07]

So this gives you, you or your sales team, your customer service team should give you a pretty good feel who they are, what they did and how you can help them. Now what you could do from within the live chat, you can add a tag.
